perturbation.original_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement (“A greement”) is entered into by and between PromonLogicalis Tecnologia e Participações Ltda. and PTLS Serviços de Tecnologia e Assessoria Técnica Ltda., both with main office at Av. Presidente Juscelino Kubitschek, 1830, 1º andar, Torre I an d II, São Paulo, SP, Brasil , (“hereinafter referred to “PromonLogicalis ”), and the Company . PromonLogicalis and the Company are individually referred to herein as the “ Party ”, “Disclosing Part y” or “ Receiving Party ”, as appropriate, or collectively as the “ Parties ”.
perturbation.changed_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement (“A greement”) is entered into by and between PromonLogicalis Tecnologia e Participações Ltda. and PTLS Serviços de Tecnologia e Assessoria Técnica Ltda., both with main office at Av. Presidente Juscelino Kubitschek, 1830, 1º andar, Torre I an d II, São Paulo, SP, Brasil , (“hereinafter referred to “PromonLogicalis ”), and the Company . PromonLogicalis and the Company are individually referred to herein as the “ Party ”, “Releasing Part y” or “ Accessing Party ”, as appropriate, or collectively as the “ Parties ”.
perturbation.explanation: The original text identifies parties as 'Disclosing Party' or 'Receiving Party'. By changing these terms to 'Releasing Party' or 'Accessing Party,' the contract introduces ambiguity. These new terms lack legal precedent in the context of NDAs and make it unclear about what exactly their obligations are, creating an in-text contradiction with how Disclosing and Receiving party responsibilities are already outlined in the agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party undertakes, for the period of time speci fied in Section 13, to keep confidential all Confidential Information submitted by the Disclosing Party , either directly or indirectly through subcontractors and other partners of the Disclosing Party , and being subject to the rules of intellectual property.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines parties as 'Disclosing Party' and 'Receiving Party', which are standard terms in NDAs with established legal meaning. The changed text replaces these with 'Releasing Party' and 'Accessing Party'. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text references 'Receiving Party' and 'Disclosing Party'. Since the 'Receiving Party' and 'Disclosing Party' are now undefined, the obligation outlined in the contradicted text becomes unclear and unenforceable, creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition.

perturbation.original_text: All the information that (i) i s written and marked as CONFID ENTIAL, or (ii) is disclosed verbally, and at the time of its disclosure, the Disclosing Party's identify such information to be protected in accordance with t his Agreement ( "Confidential Information" ), shall be considered confidenti al and restricted property of the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.changed_text: All the information that (i) i s written and marked as CONFID ENTIAL, or (ii) is disclosed verbally, and at the time of its disclosure, the Disclosing Party's identify such information to be protected in accordance with t his Agreement ( "Proprietary Details" ), shall be considered confidenti al and restricted property of the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation changes the term 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Details'. This introduces an in-text contradiction because the rest of the agreement refers to 'Confidential Information', and now the definition in this section creates uncertainty as to whether 'Proprietary Details' is equivalent and if all clauses that refers to Confidential Information also applies to Proprietary Details.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees for the period specified in Section 13 that it will: a. Restrict disclosure of the Confidential Information to the minimum required number of people (such as employees, directors, office rs, legal counsels, accountants or consultants who shall also be personally bound to maintain the Information in confidence); b. Direct its employees, agents and subcontractors to abide by non-disclosure terms at least as protective of the Confidential Informati on as those set forth herein; c. Maintain the confidentiality a nd take proper measures to pro tect the secrecy of the Confidential Information , using the same degree of care t hat it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in any event shall us e at least commercially reasonable care .; d. Immediately notify the other party of any misappropriation o r misuse of Confidential Information .
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Details' in the definition, while the contradicted text refers to 'Confidential Information.' This creates a direct contradiction because the contradicted text explicitly outlines obligations regarding 'Confidential Information,' and now there's ambiguity as to whether those obligations also apply to 'Proprietary Details.' This misalignment in terminology introduces uncertainty and a potential in-text contradiction as outlined in the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: The term "Information" shall include all written information , verbal or otherwise presented in tangible or intangible way, including, but n ot limited to, trade secrets, d iscoveries, ideas, concepts, know-how, techniques, technology, products, designs, specifications, drawings, diagra ms, data, computer programs, business activities and operations, repo rts, studies and other technical , commercial, financial in formation of each Party or its Affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Data" shall include all written information , verbal or otherwise presented in tangible or intangible way, including, but n ot limited to, trade secrets, d iscoveries, ideas, concepts, know-how, techniques, technology, products, designs, specifications, drawings, diagra ms, data, computer programs, business activities and operations, repo rts, studies and other technical , commercial, financial in formation of each Party or its Affiliates.
perturbation.explanation: Here, the term 'Information' is replaced by 'Data'. This creates an in-text contradiction. The agreement extensively uses 'Information', and changing the definition to 'Data' in this isolated section implies that only data, as specifically understood, is covered, while other types of information might not be subject to the confidentiality clauses of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All information (either Confidential Information or not) fur nished under this Agreement shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and shall be returned to it or destroyed promptly at its reque st (together with all relevant copies, extracts, plan s, schematics or other reproduct ions). ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED “AS IS.” NEITHER PARTY MAKES ANY WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING THE ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE OF THE CONF IDENTIAL INFORMATION, AND EACH PARTY EXPRESSLY DISCLAIMS ANY WARRANTY OF MERCHANTABILITY, NON-INFRINGEMENT OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines "Information", while the changed text defines "Data". The contradicted text refers to "information (either Confidential Information or not)". By changing the definition from "Information" to "Data", the modified text creates an in-text contradiction, as the agreement extensively uses 'Information', and the change implies that only 'Data', is now covered, while other types of information might not be, creating ambiguity about which information is covered under the clause in the contradicted text, according to the Perturbation Explanation. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party undertakes, for the period of time speci fied in Section 13, to keep confidential all Confidential Information submitted by the Disclosing Party , either directly or indirectly through subcontractors and other partners of the Disclosing Party , and being subject to the rules of intellectual property.
perturbation.changed_text: The Accessing Entity undertakes, for the period of time speci fied in Section 13, to keep confidential all Confidential Information submitted by the Disclosing Party , either directly or indirectly through subcontractors and other partners of the Disclosing Party , and being subject to the rules of intellectual property.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Receiving Party' to 'Accessing Entity' only in this section creates an in-text contradiction. Previously, in the definition, Receiving Party was already defined, however, now the agreement now refers to it as Accessing Entity in a single sentence, creating ambiguity about the scope of this entities liability and relationship with the 'Receiving Party' defined in the initial paragraph, which now is 'Accessing Party'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party for purposes of confidentiality is bound by its managers, emplo yees, agents, for any purpose, and principals.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Receiving Party' with 'Accessing Entity' in one section of the contract. The 'Contradicted Text' defines 'Receiving Party', which now clashes with the 'Changed Text' that refers to the same entity as 'Accessing Entity'. Because the contract now refers to the Receiving Party as Accessing Entity, a new, undefined term is introduced when it had already been defined. This constitutes a strong, in-text contradiction according to the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Maintain the confidentiality a nd take proper measures to pro tect the secrecy of the Confidential Information , using the same degree of care t hat it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in any event shall us e at least commercially reasonable care .
perturbation.changed_text: Maintain the confidentiality a nd take proper measures to pro tect the secrecy of the Confidential Information , using the same degree of care t hat it uses to protect its own internal data, but in any event shall us e at least commercially reasonable care .
perturbation.explanation: Here, 'confidential information' is changed to 'internal data' within the phrase 'its own confidential information'. This introduces an in-text contradiction as it suggests a different standard of care. 'Internal data' is not defined, and this implies a lower degree of protection for the Disclosing Party's information compared to the Receiving Party's own 'confidential information' as was the previously defined metric.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Direct its employees, agents and subcontractors to abide by non-disclosure terms at least as protective of the Confidential Informati on as those set forth herein
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'internal data' within the phrase addressing the standard of care for protecting information. This introduces an in-text contradiction because 'internal data' is undefined, and thus implies a potentially lower standard of protection than 'Confidential Information' as defined elsewhere. The contradicted text requires employees to abide by non-disclosure terms at least as protective as those defined for 'Confidential Information'. By creating ambiguity around the standard of protection for the Receiving Party's own data, the changed text undermines the requirement that employee non-disclosure agreements must be 'at least as protective' of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All information (either Confidential Information or not) fur nished under this Agreement shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and shall be returned to it or destroyed promptly at its reque st (together with all relevant copies, extracts, plan s, schematics or other reproduct ions).
perturbation.changed_text: All documentation (either Confidential Information or not) fur nished under this Agreement shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and shall be returned to it or destroyed promptly at its reque st (together with all relevant copies, extracts, plan s, schematics or other reproduct ions).
perturbation.explanation: Changing the word 'information' to 'documentation' in this section creates an in-text contradiction. The agreement defines 'Information' broadly. Changing it to 'documentation' narrows the scope of what must be returned/destroyed. This implies a narrower definition of 'documentation' which contrasts with the broader definition of 'Information' elsewhere, which can be inclusive of Documentation and other types of confidential data and information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term "Information" shall include all written information , verbal or otherwise presented in tangible or intangible way, including, but n ot limited to, trade secrets, d iscoveries, ideas, concepts, know-how, techniques, technology, products, designs, specifications, drawings, diagra ms, data, computer programs, business activities and operations, repo rts, studies and other technical , commercial, financial in formation of each Party or its Affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The original text used 'information' which, as defined in the contradicted text, encompasses a very broad range of items. By changing 'information' to 'documentation', the modified text narrows the scope of what must be returned or destroyed. This directly contradicts the intention of the agreement as a whole, as the Disclosing Party expects all forms of 'information' to be returned, not just documentation. This change creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement requires all 'information' to be returned, but now only 'documentation' must be returned, leading to a conflict and confusion for the contract's application.
